texto: Everything in contemporary society discourages interiority. More and more of our exchanges take place via circuits, and in their very nature those interactions are such as to keep us hovering in the virtual now, a place away from ourselves.
explicacao: Citação de Sven Birkerts sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Everything in contemporary society discourages interiority. More and more of our exchanges take p…” articula reflexao, reading, place em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e reflexao.

frase_original: Everything in contemporary society discourages interiority. More and more of our exchanges take place via circuits, and in their very nature those interactions are such as to keep us hovering in the virtual now, a place away from ourselves.

texto: I often find that a novel, even a well-written and compelling novel, can become a blur to me soon after I've finished reading it. I recollect perfectly the feeling of reading it, the mood I occupied, but I am less sure about the narrative details. It is almost as if the book were, as Wittgenstein said of his propositions, a ladder to be climbed and then discarded after it has served its purpose.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sven Birkerts: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I often find that a novel, even a well-written and compelling novel, can become a blur to me soon…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, books, novels pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de novels e reading.
